text.title: Status Report Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying status-report-templates-by-audience. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Open-rate of weekly status drops below 30% by week 4; stakeholders ask in standups "what's happening?".
antipattern.root-cause: Single 1400-word report sent to every audience; CEO doesn't scroll, PMO can't find RAG, sponsor misses blockers, VP gets jargon.
antipattern.fix: Split into 4 audience-mapped templates from a single source spreadsheet. Reduce CEO copy to 5 lines.

antipattern.symptom: CEO report says "On plan" while PMO report shows AMBER on a workstream; clients lose trust in the PM.
antipattern.root-cause: Reports were hand-edited independently, no shared source. Each one drifted to a different RAG state.
antipattern.fix: Generate all four from one spreadsheet (or script the generation). Edits go to the source; reports rebuild.

antipattern.symptom: Internal VP forwards the report to their boss with the message "I have no idea what this means".
antipattern.root-cause: Leadership template copied technical sponsor text instead of translating to business impact.
antipattern.fix: Force leadership template to use business-impact phrases only ("Auth migration completed; users SSO-enabled; no support tickets") with no component names.

antipattern.symptom: PMO repeatedly asks "what's the RAG this week?" after the report is sent.
antipattern.root-cause: PMO template led with prose; RAG table was at the bottom or absent.
antipattern.fix: Lead with RAG table in PMO template; Green rows take no prose; Amber/Red get a sentence each.
